seoTitle: How to Transfer Outlook Emails to a New Computer (2026)
metaDescription: Move your entire Outlook mailbox, folders, and calendar to a new PC by exporting a PST backup file and importing it on your new machine.
intro: Setting up a new computer shouldn't mean losing years of email history and folder structure. Outlook lets you export your complete mailbox into a single backup file that you can transfer to your new machine and import, bringing your messages, folders, and contacts along with it.

steps.heading: Adjust sync settings to include full history
steps.body: Before exporting, check your mailbox sync slider and set it to download your complete historical backlog rather than just recent months.

steps.heading: Launch the Import/Export wizard
steps.body: Open the Import/Export tool from Outlook's file menu and choose the option to export to a file.

steps.heading: Choose the PST format and include subfolders
steps.body: Select the Outlook Data File (.pst) format and make sure the checkbox to include subfolders is checked so nothing gets left behind.

steps.heading: Save and transfer the backup file
steps.body: Choose a save location for your backup, then move the finished .pst file to your new computer using an external drive or shared network location.

steps.heading: Import on your new computer
steps.body: Open Outlook on your new machine, run the import procedure with your transferred file, and check that your folders populate correctly.

faq.question: Will I lose any emails during the transfer?
faq.answer: As long as you set your sync slider to include full history and check the include subfolders option before exporting, your complete history should transfer intact.

faq.question: What file format does Outlook use for this kind of backup?
faq.answer: Outlook uses the .pst format, which bundles your mail, folders, and often your calendar and contacts into one portable file.

faq.question: How do I physically move the backup file to a new computer?
faq.answer: You can copy it to an external hard drive, USB stick, or a shared network folder, then access it from your new computer.

faq.question: Does this method also transfer my calendar and contacts?
faq.answer: Yes, the PST export process typically captures your entire local data structure, including calendar entries and address book contacts, not just email.
